content.data: APP-BOUNDARY-101 is accepted. The web client is the primary product, and the Rust/WASM engine remains the authoritative browser solver. A second solver implementation in the UI language is prohibited.

label: solver_boundary_claim
statement: APP-BOUNDARY-101: the Rust/WASM engine is the authoritative browser solver, and no UI-language solver fork is permitted.

evidence.quote: The web client is the primary product, and the Rust/WASM engine remains the authoritative browser solver. A second solver implementation in the UI language is prohibited.

content.data: SIZE-GUARD-220 sets a hard 24,000,000-byte raw module guard. The independent 6,000,000-byte gzip target remains unchanged. Passing the raw guard does not accept the renderer, and gzip evidence must use a fresh cold-cache browser profile.

label: size_budget_claim
statement: SIZE-GUARD-220: the renderer has a 24,000,000-byte raw guard, while the independent 6,000,000-byte gzip target remains; a raw pass is not renderer acceptance and gzip evidence requires a fresh cold cache.

evidence.quote: SIZE-GUARD-220 sets a hard 24,000,000-byte raw module guard. The independent 6,000,000-byte gzip target remains unchanged. Passing the raw guard does not accept the renderer, and gzip evidence must use a fresh cold-cache browser profile.

content.data: SNAPSHOT-SEP-17 keeps project snapshots and engine-session snapshots as separate versioned artifacts. A project must remain durable and loadable without a compatible runtime snapshot.

label: snapshot_boundary_claim
statement: SNAPSHOT-SEP-17: project snapshots and runtime-session snapshots are separate, and a project remains loadable without a compatible runtime snapshot.

evidence.quote: project snapshots and engine-session snapshots as separate versioned artifacts. A project must remain durable and loadable without a compatible runtime snapshot.

content.data: LIMITS-RUNTIME-44 supplies resource limits per execution. Protocol and solver interfaces must not encode teaching-scale ceilings, dense-only matrices, fixed timesteps, first-order-only integration, or the current component catalog.

label: resource_limits_claim
statement: LIMITS-RUNTIME-44: execution supplies resource limits; protocol and solver interfaces cannot bake in teaching-scale ceilings, dense-only matrices, fixed timesteps, first-order-only integration, or the current component catalog.

evidence.quote: LIMITS-RUNTIME-44 supplies resource limits per execution. Protocol and solver interfaces must not encode teaching-scale ceilings, dense-only matrices, fixed timesteps, first-order-only integration, or the current component catalog.

content.data: CANVAS-OFF-31 requires that removing or disabling the geometry canvas still leaves project loading, graph transactions, compilation, analysis, persistence snapshots, and CLI operation usable.

label: canvas_disabled_claim
statement: CANVAS-OFF-31: disabling the geometry canvas must preserve project loading, graph transactions, compilation, analysis, persistence snapshots, and CLI operation.

evidence.quote: removing or disabling the geometry canvas still leaves project loading, graph transactions, compilation, analysis, persistence snapshots, and CLI operation usable

content.data: OPT-HYP-09 reduced raw bytes and preserved the static ABI, but gzip remained 12 percent over target. Browser startup, runtime, visual, and lifecycle behavior were unmeasured. The artifact was not adopted and the raw guard did not ratchet down.

label: optimization_rejected_claim
statement: OPT-HYP-09 was not adopted: it preserved the ABI and reduced raw size but missed gzip by 12 percent, left browser behavior unmeasured, and did not ratchet the raw guard.

evidence.quote: OPT-HYP-09 reduced raw bytes and preserved the static ABI, but gzip remained 12 percent over target. Browser startup, runtime, visual, and lifecycle behavior were unmeasured. The artifact was not adopted and the raw guard did not ratchet down.

content.data: VERIFY-PHASE-6 passed 142 native and portable tests plus architecture enforcement. Authenticated browser E2E, visual fidelity, lifecycle soak, and measured performance gates remain open.

label: verification_open_claim
statement: VERIFY-PHASE-6 passed 142 native and portable tests, but authenticated browser E2E, visual, lifecycle soak, and measured performance gates remain open.

evidence.quote: VERIFY-PHASE-6 passed 142 native and portable tests plus architecture enforcement. Authenticated browser E2E, visual fidelity, lifecycle soak, and measured performance gates remain open.

content.data: ACK-OWNER-72 assigns sole ownership of the exact terminal acknowledgement to the engine session. Adapters may observe the acknowledgement but cannot originate or replace it.

label: ack_owner_claim
statement: ACK-OWNER-72: the engine session alone owns the exact terminal acknowledgement; adapters can observe it but cannot originate or replace it.

evidence.quote: ACK-OWNER-72 assigns sole ownership of the exact terminal acknowledgement to the engine session. Adapters may observe the acknowledgement but cannot originate or replace it.

content.data: PRELOAD-JS-53 uses a retryable single-flight preload for renderer JavaScript glue only. Canvas startup reuses it. The preload never initializes WASM, the simulation engine, or a Worker.

label: preload_boundary_claim
statement: PRELOAD-JS-53 preloads only renderer JavaScript glue and never initializes WASM, the simulation engine, or a Worker.

evidence.quote: PRELOAD-JS-53 uses a retryable single-flight preload for renderer JavaScript glue only. Canvas startup reuses it. The preload never initializes WASM, the simulation engine, or a Worker.

content.data: RENDER-OWNER-88 commits the retained canvas to domain geometry. The UI framework owns product chrome, panels, controls, routing, responsive layout, themes, and accessibility.

label: ui_ownership_claim
statement: RENDER-OWNER-88: the retained canvas owns domain geometry, while the UI framework owns chrome, panels, controls, routing, responsive layout, themes, and accessibility.

evidence.quote: RENDER-OWNER-88 commits the retained canvas to domain geometry. The UI framework owns product chrome, panels, controls, routing, responsive layout, themes, and accessibility.
